page: Amazon Web Services r8idn.metal-96xl is a memory-optimized bare metal server powered by Intel processors. It features an x86_64 architecture with 384 virtual CPUs across 192 physical cores, utilizing a dedicated allocation model with two threads per core. The processors operate at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, delivering high single-core and multi-core compute capabilities.

The server is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core to support large-scale in-memory computations. For local storage, it includes 22800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-speed read and write capabilities directly on the host. Network and EBS performance are optimized, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, and high-throughput data processing. The combination of massive RAM, high-speed local NVMe storage, and high-bandwidth networking supports demanding enterprise applications, real-time analytics, and in-memory databases without virtualization overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idn.metal-96xl is a memory-optimized bare metal server designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. It features 384 vCPUs across 192 physical Intel cores with a 3.9 GHz clock speed on an x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of RAM, offering 8.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 22800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Optimized for high-throughput networking, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. With no integrated GPUs, this bare metal instance is built for massive in-memory databases, large-scale data processing, and network-heavy workloads requiring dedicated hardware performance.
